statement.title: Statement
statement.content:
Description
Vadim's hourglass measures $ s $ minutes. He flipped it over, and the time started. Every $ k $ minutes, Vadim will flip his hourglass again. He will do this even if the sand is still falling. Also, if the sand has already finished falling but there are still some minutes left, Vadim will wait for the required time and only then flip the hourglass. Each flip is done instantly.

However, Vadim needs to leave for errands in $ m $ minutes, and he will stop flipping the hourglass (if Vadim needs to flip the hourglass in the last minute, he will do so). Determine how many minutes the sand will continue to fall after Vadim leaves.

Input Format
Each test consists of several test cases. The first line contains a single integer $ t $ $ (1 \le t \le 10^4) $ — the number of test cases. The following lines describe the test cases.

In a single line of each test case, there are three integers $ s $ , $ k $ , and $ m $ — the number of minutes the hourglass measures, the number of minutes after which the hourglass flips, and the number of minutes after which Vadim will leave for errands $ (1 \le s, k, m \le 10^9) $ .

Output Format
For each test case, output how many minutes the sand will continue to fall after Vadim leaves.

Examples
Sample 1
Input
6
8 8 12
5 10 17
12 2 3
16 7 7
1 1 10
2 60 15
Output
4
0
1
7
1
0

Note
In the first test case, Vadim will flip the hourglass just as the sand stops falling, then $ 4 $ minutes pass, Vadim leaves, and $ 4 $ minutes will remain.

In the second test case, Vadim will flip the hourglass at the $ 10 $ -th minute, in the next $ 5 $ minutes the sand will completely finish falling, and in $ 2 $ minutes Vadim will leave, resulting in $ 0 $ minutes remaining.

In the third test case, $ 2 $ minutes of sand have already fallen before the first flip, so after the flip, this sand will continue to fall. After $ 1 $ minute, $ 1 $ minute will remain.

In the fourth test case, $ 7 $ minutes of sand have already fallen before the first flip, Vadim will perform it and immediately leave, so $ 7 $ minutes will remain.

hints.title: Hint
hints.content:
How many minutes of sand will remain in the upper half of the hourglass immediately after the second flip?
Answer
$$$s$$$ minutes.

solutions.title: Editorial
solutions.content:
2184B - Hourglass
For now, we will not consider $$$m$$$. Let's note two cases:
If $$$s  \gt  k$$$, then in the first $$$k$$$ minutes, $$$k$$$ minutes of sand will fall out, and there will be $$$s-k$$$ minutes of sand left in the upper half of the hourglass. After the first flip, there will be $$$k$$$ minutes of sand on top. This means that in the first $$$2 \cdot k$$$ minutes, there will be nothing left in the upper half. From this, we can conclude that every $$$2 \cdot k$$$ minutes, the amount of sand in the upper half will be the same.
If $$$s \le k$$$, then in the first $$$k$$$ minutes, all $$$s$$$ minutes of sand will fall out, and there will be nothing left in the upper half. From this, we can conclude that every $$$k$$$ minutes, the amount of sand in the upper half will be the same.
Now let's return to $$$m$$$. We will consider both cases:
If $$$s  \gt  k$$$, replace the value of $$$m$$$ with $$$m \mod {2 \cdot k}$$$. If $$$0 \le m \le k-1$$$, then there will be $$$s - m$$$ minutes of sand left in the upper half, and if $$$k \le m \le 2\cdot k - 1$$$, then there will be $$$k - (m - k)$$$ minutes of sand left in the upper half.
If $$$s \le k$$$, replace the value of $$$m$$$ with $$$m \mod {k}$$$. If $$$0 \le m  \lt  s$$$, then there will be $$$s - m$$$ minutes of sand left in the upper half; otherwise, there will be $$$0$$$ minutes of sand left.
